knowledge_objects.name: Vector Space
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A set of objects called vectors that can be added together and multiplied by scalars while satisfying the vector space axioms.
knowledge_objects.source_span: A vector space is a set of objects called vectors that can be added together and multiplied by scalars while satisfying the vector space axioms.

knowledge_objects.name: Basis
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A set of independent vectors that can represent every vector in the space through linear combinations.
knowledge_objects.source_span: A basis is a set of independent vectors that can represent every vector in the space through linear combinations.

knowledge_objects.name: Dimension
knowledge_objects.short_definition: The number of vectors in a basis of a vector space.
knowledge_objects.source_span: The number of vectors in a basis is the dimension of the vector space.

knowledge_objects.name: Matrix
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A rectangular array of numbers that can represent a linear transformation once bases have been chosen for the input and output spaces.
knowledge_objects.source_span: A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers that can represent a linear transformation once bases have been chosen for the input and output spaces.

knowledge_objects.name: Linear Transformation
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A transformation represented by a matrix once bases have been chosen for the input and output spaces.
knowledge_objects.source_span: a linear transformation once bases have been chosen for the input and output spaces

knowledge_objects.name: Matrix Multiplication
knowledge_objects.short_definition: An operation that corresponds to composing linear transformations.
knowledge_objects.source_span: Matrix multiplication corresponds to composing linear transformations

knowledge_objects.name: Eigenvector
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A nonzero vector whose direction is preserved by the transformation represented by a square matrix.
knowledge_objects.source_span: For a square matrix \(A\), an eigenvector is a nonzero vector \(v\) whose direction is preserved by the transformation represented by \(A\).

knowledge_objects.name: Eigenvalue
knowledge_objects.short_definition: The scalar factor by which an eigenvector is scaled under a linear transformation.

knowledge_objects.name: Characteristic Polynomial
knowledge_objects.short_definition: The polynomial \(\det(\lambda I - A)\) whose roots are the eigenvalues of a square matrix.
knowledge_objects.source_span: Eigenvalues are connected to the characteristic polynomial \(\det(\lambda I - A)\).

knowledge_objects.name: Eigenvalue Equation
knowledge_objects.short_definition: The equation \(Av = \lambda v\) defining eigenvalues and eigenvectors.
